title: Core: Simplify identity module for stateless agents
description:
Simplify the identity module since agents are now stateless.

## Current Implementation (src/core/identity.rs)
- resolve_agent() - checks env var, falls back to state file
- has_identity() - checks if identity exists
- format_export() - formats export command

## New Implementation
- resolve_agent() - just reads BOTBUS_AGENT env var, returns Option<String>
- require_agent() - reads env var, returns Result with helpful error if not set
- Remove state file fallback logic

## Remove
- Any reference to per-project agent state files
- Legacy identity resolution from .botbus/

## Keep
- format_export() - still useful for help messages
- Name validation logic (is_valid_agent_name)

## Example
```rust
pub fn resolve_agent() -> Option<String> {
    std::env::var("BOTBUS_AGENT").ok()
}

pub fn require_agent() -> Result<String> {
    resolve_agent().ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(
        "BOTBUS_AGENT environment variable not set.\n\n\
         Set your identity:\n  \
         export BOTBUS_AGENT=\"
    ))
}
```
close_reason: Completed in XDG migration

close_reason: Fixed: Claim extension messages now post to #claims instead of #general

title: Add 'channels close|reopen' commands
description:
Implement channel close/reopen commands to hide channels from listings and UI without deleting data.

**Closed channel behavior:**
- Hidden from `botbus channels list` (unless --all flag used)
- Hidden from TUI channel list
- Can still be accessed explicitly if needed
- Preserves all message history and state
- Stored in state.json or similar metadata

**Commands to add:**
- `botbus channels close <channel>` - Mark channel as closed
- `botbus channels reopen <channel>` - Unmark channel
- `botbus channels list --all` - Show closed channels (grayed out or marked)

**Implementation notes:**
- Store closed channel list in state.json
- Update channels::run() to filter out closed channels
- Update TUI to filter closed channels
- Consider adding a 'status' field to channel listings

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Closed channels don't appear in default listings
- Closed channels appear with --all flag
- Messages can still be read from closed channels
- Tests cover close/reopen workflow
close_reason: Completed: implemented close/reopen commands with state tracking and TUI integration

title: Epic: Migrate BotBus to global XDG-based storage
description:
Migrate BotBus from per-project .botbus/ directories to a global ~/.local/share/botbus/ installation.

## Motivation
- Per-project storage breaks with git worktrees/workspaces (causes merge conflicts)
- No cross-project communication possible
- Requires manual copy-paste to coordinate agents across projects

## New Architecture
- Single global storage at $XDG_DATA_HOME/botbus/ (~/.local/share/botbus/)
- Channels are global, scoped by naming convention (e.g., #projectname, #projectname.topic)
- Claims use absolute paths (auto-expanded from cwd)
- Agent identity is global and persistent

## Breaking Changes
- Remove botbus init command (or repurpose for project-specific setup)
- Remove --project flag from all commands
- Claims now require absolute paths internally
- Channels are no longer project-scoped by directory

## Version
Bump to 0.2.0
close_reason: XDG migration complete

title: Add --limit and --since flags to claims command
description:
Add filtering options to 'botbus claims' command:

- --limit/-n <N>: Limit output to N most recent claims (sorted by creation time)
- --since <timespec>: Only show claims created after given time (e.g., '2h ago', '2026-01-28')

This helps when claims.jsonl grows large and users want to see recent activity without scrolling through hundreds of historical claims.

Similar to history command which already has -n/--limit flag.

Implementation notes:
- Claims are already sorted by expires_at; may want to add sort by created_at option
- --since should accept relative times (2h, 1d) and absolute timestamps
- Flags should work with --all and --mine filters

title: Support URI-based claims for non-file resources
description:
## Summary

Extend claims beyond file paths to support resource URIs - any addressable thing an agent might want exclusive access to (beads, database tables, API endpoints, etc.).

## Motivation

Currently claims only work for files. But agents coordinate around more than files:
- Multiple agents might work on related issues
- An agent doing a database migration wants to "lock" certain tables
- An agent spinning up a dev server wants to claim a port

## Proposed Design

### URI Schemes

```
file:///path/to/file.rs        # Explicit file (optional)
bead://project/bd-xxx          # A specific issue  
db://myapp/users               # A database table
port://8080                    # A local port
```

### Usage

```bash
# Claim a bead
botbus claim "bead://botbus/bd-111" -m "Working on this issue"

# Claim with wildcard
botbus claim "bead://botbus/*" -m "Major refactor"

# Check before working
botbus check-claim "bead://botbus/bd-111"
```

### Implementation Notes

1. Claims are opaque strings - BotBus doesn't interpret URI semantics
2. Conflict detection uses prefix/glob matching (same as files)
3. Bare paths (no scheme) remain files for backward compatibility
4. Display groups claims by scheme in `botbus claims` output

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `botbus claim` accepts URI strings (not just file paths)
- [ ] `botbus check-claim` works with URIs
- [ ] `botbus claims` displays URIs nicely (grouped by scheme)
- [ ] Backward compatible - bare paths still work as files
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Out of Scope (for now)

- Validating URI schemes
- Integrating with external tools (e.g., checking if bead exists)
- Auto-prefixing file paths with file://
close_reason: Implemented URI claims feature

title: inbox: fix --limit-per-channel + --mark-read interaction
description:
When using --limit-per-channel with --mark-read, the command marks ALL fetched messages as read (up to --count limit), not just the displayed messages.

Example: 50 unread messages, --limit-per-channel 5 --mark-read:
- Shows 5 messages
- Marks all 50 as read
- Agent misses 45 messages

Fix: When --limit-per-channel is used with --mark-read, only mark as read up to the last message ID that was actually displayed. Track the last displayed message per channel and use its offset/ID for marking.

Alternative: Document that these flags conflict and error out when used together.
close_reason: Fixed: --limit-per-channel now correctly marks only displayed messages as read using message ID tracking

title: Design: Agent naming and registration conventions
description:
Design the agent naming system for global BotBus.

## Decision: No Registration, Stateless Identity

BotBus is stateless about agent identity. It trusts whatever name is passed via BOTBUS_AGENT env var.

## Key Principles
1. Agent identity is like an IRC nick - stable, not role-based
2. An agent might work on any project, any task - name doesn't imply role
3. Orchestrator/user is responsible for name persistence across restarts
4. BotBus just tags messages/claims with the provided name

## Implementation

### Required
- `BOTBUS_AGENT` env var must be set for all commands that need identity
- Error with helpful message if not set

### Commands
- `botbus generate-name` - Returns random kebab-case name, no side effects
- `botbus whoami` - Prints $BOTBUS_AGENT or error if not set
- `botbus agents` - Derives agent list from message/claim history (no agents.jsonl)

### Removed
- `botbus register` - no registration ceremony
- `agents.jsonl` - no central agent registry
- Join messages - no 'X has joined' announcements

## Name Format: kebab-case (IRC style)
Examples:
- crimson-fox
- silent-oak  
- swift-raven
- quiet-badger

NOT:
- CrimsonFox (PascalCase)
- crimson_fox (snake_case)
- CRIMSON-FOX (SCREAMING)

## Validation
- Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens
- 1-64 chars
- Must start with letter
- No consecutive hyphens
- No leading/trailing hyphens

## Name Generation
Update names crate usage or generate manually:
```rust
fn generate_name() -> String {
    let adj = random_adjective();  // "crimson", "silent", "swift"
    let noun = random_noun();       // "fox", "oak", "raven"
    format!("{}-{}", adj, noun)
}
```

## Example Workflow
```bash
# Orchestrator spawns agent with stable identity
export BOTBUS_AGENT=$(botbus generate-name)  # -> quiet-river
# Orchestrator persists this name for restarts

# Agent works across projects freely
botbus send botbus 'Picking up bd-123'
botbus send botcrit 'Done with botbus, moving here'

# Looks like IRC:
# <quiet-river> Picking up bd-123
# <swift-raven> On it, will review when done
```
close_reason: Implemented: stateless identity model with BOTBUS_AGENT env var, generate-name command, kebab-case names

title: Agents should not see their own messages as unread in inbox
description:
Feature request from claude:

Currently an agent's own sent messages (e.g., 'Completed bd-xxx') show up as unread for that same agent. This causes false positives in work-detection loops — has_work() sees unread inbox messages and spawns a claude session that finds no actual work.

Observed in agent-loop.sh eval: storm-raven's own completion messages triggered a wasted iteration.

Solution: Filter out the current agent's own messages when showing inbox unread counts and messages.

Implementation notes:
- Modify inbox command to exclude messages where message.agent == current_agent
- Applies to both --count-only and regular inbox display
- Should work across all channels
close_reason: Implemented inbox filtering to exclude agent's own messages

title: Feature: show date and time in channel history text output
description:
Currently botbus history shows just time (HH:MM), but not the date.

Current output:
  [14:31] botbus-dev: message

This makes it hard to tell if a message is from today, yesterday, or last week.

Desired output:
  [2026-01-31 14:31] botbus-dev: message

Or relative dates for recent messages:
  [Today 14:31] botbus-dev: message
  [Yesterday 15:12] botbox-dev: message
  [2026-01-29 10:00] other-agent: message

Note: This only applies to TOON format output. JSON format already includes full timestamps.
close_reason: Implemented: history text output now shows relative dates (Today/Yesterday) and full dates for older messages

title: CLI: Remove init command
description:
Remove the init command since botbus is now global.

## Current Behavior
`botbus init` creates .botbus/ directory structure in a project.

## New Behavior
- Remove init command entirely
- Data directory is auto-created on first use via ensure_data_dir()
- No per-project initialization needed

## Changes
- Remove src/cli/init.rs
- Remove Init variant from Commands enum in src/cli/mod.rs
- Remove init handling from src/main.rs
- Update ensure_data_dir() to create structure on first use

## Alternative: Repurpose?
Could repurpose for 'botbus init' to:
- Create general channel with welcome message
- Print setup instructions

But probably not needed - simpler to just auto-create on first command.

## Migration Note
Existing .botbus/ directories in projects are now ignored.
Add note to migration guide (bd-3s0).

title: Add channel hooks: auto-spawn agents on bus send
description:
## Summary

Channel hooks: when a message is sent to a channel, evaluate conditions and optionally execute a command. Enables auto-spawning agents when work arrives.

## API Design

### Adding hooks

Command is a vector (everything after `--`). No shell expansion, direct execvp.

```
bus hooks add \
  --channel botbus \
  --if-claim-available "agent://botbus-dev" \
  --cwd /home/bob/src/botbus \
  --cooldown 30s \
  -- bash scripts/dev-loop.sh botbus botbus-dev
```

Options:
- `--channel <channel>` — which channel triggers this hook
- `--if-claim-available <claim>` — only fire if this claim URI is not held
- `--cwd <directory>` — working directory for the command (validated at add-time)
- `--cooldown <duration>` — minimum time between firings (default 30s)
- `--` — everything after this is the command vector

### Managing hooks

Each hook gets a terse ID with `hk` prefix (e.g. `hk-12d`). Will migrate to terseid library when available. Convention: prefer at least 2-char prefixes for all ID namespaces.

```
bus hooks list                  # show all configured hooks
bus hooks remove <hook-id>      # delete a hook
bus hooks test <hook-id>        # dry-run: evaluate condition, show what would execute
```

### Execution flow (on `bus send`)

1. Write the message (as normal)
2. Check if any hooks match the target channel
3. For each matching hook, evaluate the condition (`--if-claim-available`)
4. Check cooldown — skip if fired too recently
5. If condition passes, spawn command in background (fire-and-forget)
6. Append to audit log

## Security Requirements

**Critical: this is arbitrary command execution triggered by messages.**

1. **No content interpolation.** Message body, agent name, channel name are NEVER interpolated into the command. The command vector is static — exactly what was specified at add-time.
2. **Command stored as vector, not string.** No shell interpretation. Executed via direct execvp, not `sh -c`.
3. **Cooldown is mandatory.** Default 30s. The `--if-claim-available` guard is also a natural rate limiter (spawned process takes the claim, preventing re-fire).
4. **Hooks are local-only config.** Stored in `$BOTBUS_DATA_DIR/hooks/`, not in channel metadata. Two machines watching the same channel can have completely different hooks. Only the local user can add hooks.
5. **`--cwd` validated at add-time.** Directory must exist and be accessible when the hook is created.
6. **Audit log.** Every hook evaluation appended to `$BOTBUS_DATA_DIR/hooks/audit.log`: timestamp, channel, message ID, hook ID, condition result, whether command was executed, exit code (if available).

## Storage

Hook config in `$BOTBUS_DATA_DIR/hooks/`:
- `hooks.toml` or `hooks.json` — hook definitions indexed by hook ID
- `audit.log` — append-only fire log

## Future (phase 2)

- `--if-no-agent <name>` — higher-level predicate: fire if no agent with this name has been active recently. Simpler than knowing the claim URI convention.
- General hooks with more trigger types (e.g., on-claim, on-release)
- `bus daemon` / `bus watch --auto-spawn` for liveness monitoring and crash recovery
- Migrate hook IDs to terseid library (prefix: `hk`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bus hooks add --channel <ch> --if-claim-available <claim> --cwd <dir> -- <cmd...>` persists hook config
- Hook gets a terse ID with `hk` prefix (e.g. `hk-12d`)
- `bus hooks list` shows all hooks with IDs, channels, conditions, commands
- `bus hooks remove <id>` deletes a hook
- `bus hooks test <id>` dry-runs condition evaluation without executing
- `bus send` to a hooked channel evaluates hooks and spawns if conditions pass
- Spawn is background (sender not blocked)
- Every hook evaluation logged to audit.log
- Command executed as vector via execvp, no shell interpretation
- Cooldown enforced (default 30s)
- `--cwd` validated at add-time
close_reason: Implemented channel hooks: add, list, remove, test subcommands with claim-available condition and audit logging

title: Add message read tracking with --after-offset and mark-read command
description:
Track which messages agents have seen to avoid re-reading.

Design (stateless + persistent fallback):
1. history --after-offset=<bytes> - agent passes offset from context
2. history --after-id=<ulid> - robust alternative using message ID
3. mark-read <channel> --offset=<bytes> - persist read cursor
4. inbox <channel> - get only unread messages (uses stored offset)

Per-agent state stored in .botbus/agents/<AgentName>/state.json to avoid conflicts when multiple agents share a project.

This helps agents avoid re-processing messages after context compaction.
close_reason: Implemented message read tracking with inbox and mark-read commands

title: Set up Cargo.toml with all dependencies

title: Show multi-line messages in TUI instead of squishing to one line
description:
Currently the TUI displays multi-line messages squished into a single line, making them hard to read.

Example: When an agent posts a message with multiple paragraphs or bullet points, the TUI shows it all on one line instead of preserving the formatting.

Desired behavior:
- Preserve line breaks in message display
- Show multi-line messages with proper formatting
- Consider wrapping long lines to fit the panel width
- Maintain readability for both single-line and multi-line messages

This affects:
- Message display in the messages panel
- DM conversations
- Any channel with formatted messages

Impact: Currently makes structured messages (like status updates with bullet points or multi-paragraph explanations) difficult to read in the TUI.

Changed format_message to return Vec<Line> instead of single Line.
Message bodies are split by newlines, with continuation lines
indented to align with the first line's message content.

Rendering example:
  [12:00] alice: First line
                 Second line
                 Third line

failures unrelated to this change).

title: Use BOTBUS_AGENT env var for identity instead of state file
description:
Current design stores 'current_agent' in .botbus/state.json, which breaks when multiple agents work from the same directory. 

Solution:
1. register command outputs: export BOTBUS_AGENT=AgentName
2. All commands read identity from BOTBUS_AGENT env var (with --agent flag override)
3. whoami reads from env var, not state file
4. Remove current_agent from state.json

This matches how mcp_agent_mail works - identity flows through the session/process, not shared state.

Agents should check: (1) only their DMs by default, or (2) use --channels to explicitly check specific channels, or (3) use --all to check everything. This prevents new agents from being overwhelmed by general channel history.

title: TUI: add 'New Messages' separator that auto-clears after viewing
description:
## Overview

Add a visual separator in the TUI message view to clearly indicate where new (unread) messages begin. The separator should:
- Display between old and new messages
- Use Unicode box drawing characters (─) with centered text
- Auto-clear after 2 seconds of viewing the messages
- Clear the (x) new messages indicator when dismissed

## Design

### Visual Example

```
[10:30] alice: Old message 1
[10:31] bob: Old message 2
─────────── New Messages ───────────
[10:45] charlie: New message 1
[10:46] dave: New message 2
```

### Behavior

1. **Show separator when:**
   - Channel has new messages (count > 0)
   - User switches to a channel with new messages
   - New messages arrive in current channel

2. **Position:**
   - Between last "old" message and first "new" message
   - Based on initial_sizes tracking

3. **Auto-clear:**
   - Start 2-second timer when channel with separator is viewed
   - After 2 seconds, remove separator and reset new message count to 0
   - Update initial_sizes to current size (mark all as read)

4. **Manual clear:**
   - User can press 'm' to manually mark all as read (existing behavior?)
   - Or just wait 2 seconds

## Implementation Notes

### Data Model Changes

In `src/tui/app.rs`:
```rust
pub struct App {
    // Existing fields...
    
    /// Timestamp when current channel was focused (for auto-clear timer)
    channel_focused_at: Option<std::time::Instant>,
    
    /// Which channels have visible separators
    separator_visible: std::collections::HashSet<String>,
}
```

### Render Logic

In message rendering (`src/tui/ui.rs`):
1. Get new message count for current channel
2. If count > 0, find separator position (based on initial_sizes)
3. Insert separator line at that position
4. Style: dimmed gray box characters with centered bold text

### Timer Logic

In event loop (`src/tui/app.rs::run_loop`):
1. When channel selected, set `channel_focused_at = Some(Instant::now())`
2. Each loop iteration, check if 2 seconds elapsed
3. If elapsed and separator visible, clear it:
   - Remove from `separator_visible`
   - Update `initial_sizes[channel]` to current size
   - Clear `new_message_counts[channel]`

### Unicode Characters

Use box drawing characters:
- Horizontal line: `─` (U+2500)
- Alternative: `━` (U+2501) for thicker line

Center text by calculating padding based on terminal width.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Separator appears between old and new messages
- [ ] Separator uses Unicode box drawing with centered text
- [ ] Separator auto-clears after 2 seconds of viewing
- [ ] New message indicator (x) clears when separator dismissed
- [ ] Separator doesn't appear if all messages are new
- [ ] Works correctly when switching between channels

## Future Enhancements (out of scope)

- Configurable timer duration
- Option to disable auto-clear
- Different styles for separator (color, thickness)
close_reason: Implemented new messages separator with auto-clear

title: Design: Message conventions and best practices
description:
Design message conventions and best practices for agent communication.

## Core Principle
Messages should be concise, actionable, and provide context for async coordination.
Feel should be like IRC - casual, lowercase, direct.

## Message Patterns

### Status Updates
```
starting bd-xyz: brief description of what you're working on
progress bd-xyz: 3/5 tests passing, debugging edge case
done bd-xyz: implemented feature, all tests passing
```

### Coordination
```
blocked: need api credentials to proceed with bd-xyz
question: should auth middleware go in src/api or src/auth?
handoff @swift-raven: bd-xyz ready for review
```

### Cross-Project
```
fyi: released botbus v0.2.0, may affect botcrit integration
@crimson-fox can you help with the database schema?
```

## Example Conversation (IRC style)
```
#botbus
<quiet-river> starting bd-123: tui scrolling fix
<swift-raven> sounds good, i'll handle bd-124 (cli cleanup)
<quiet-river> question: should scroll speed be configurable?
<swift-raven> probably not for now, keep it simple
<quiet-river> done bd-123: fixed scrolling, tests pass
<swift-raven> nice, i'll review after finishing bd-124
```

## Labels (Optional Feature)
Consider supporting labels for filtering:
```bash
botbus send botbus "fixed bug" --label=bug --label=done
botbus history --label=blocked
```

Labels: bug, feature, question, blocked, done, review, urgent

## Documentation Locations
1. AGENTS.md template - quick reference
2. README.md - overview with examples
3. `botbus send --help` - inline examples
close_reason: Message conventions documented in AGENTS.md

title: Fix state file update race condition
description:
ProjectState::update() and AgentStateManager::update() have a race condition. The lock is released between load() and save(), allowing concurrent updates to overwrite each other.

Location: src/storage/state.rs:128-136, src/storage/agent_state.rs:132-139

Fix: Hold exclusive lock across the entire read-modify-write operation.

Severity: Medium - parallel agent operations can lose state updates.
close_reason: Fixed by holding exclusive lock across entire read-modify-write operation in update() methods

title: CLI: Remove --project flag from all commands
close_reason: Completed in XDG migration

title: TUI doesn't show new channels created while watching
description:
## Problem

When `botbus ui` is running and a new channel is created (e.g., another agent runs `botbus send new-channel "hello"`), the TUI doesn't show the new channel in the channel list. It only appears after restarting the TUI.

## Expected Behavior

The TUI should automatically detect and display new channels when they're created while watching.

## Current Behavior

- TUI starts and shows existing channels
- Another agent creates a new channel
- TUI continues showing only the original channels
- Need to quit and restart TUI to see the new channel

## Reproduction Steps

```bash
# Terminal 1
export BOTBUS_AGENT=agent-1
botbus ui

# Terminal 2
export BOTBUS_AGENT=agent-2
botbus send test-channel-new "hello"

# Back to Terminal 1 - test-channel-new doesn't appear in channel list
```

## Technical Details

The TUI uses `storage::watch::watch_directory()` to monitor the channels directory, but likely:
1. Only watches for modifications to existing channel files
2. Doesn't watch for new files being created in the directory
3. Or the watch events aren't triggering a channel list refresh

## Suggested Fix

In `src/tui/app.rs`:
- Ensure file watcher monitors for CREATE events on channels directory
- When CREATE event detected, refresh the channel list
- Update `App::refresh_channels()` or equivalent to reload from disk

Files to check:
- `src/storage/watch.rs` - watch implementation
- `src/tui/app.rs` - event handling and channel list management
close_reason: Fixed - TUI now refreshes channel list on CREATE events

Depends on subscription tracking (if using default subscribed channels)

This is more CLI-conventional than comma-separated syntax. The functionality requested in the bead is fully implemented - agents can query multiple channels at once.

title: Add 'channels delete' command (admin-only)
description:
Add administrative command to permanently delete channels and clean up all associated data.

**CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS:**
1. Print warning to stdout: "WARNING: This command is for administrators only. It permanently deletes all channel data."
2. Force interactive confirmation prompt: "Type 'delete <channel-name>' to confirm:"
3. The confirmation prompt MUST block - this prevents agents from using it

**Delete operations:**
1. Delete the channel .jsonl file from channels/
2. Remove read cursors from state.json
3. Remove FTS index entries from index.sqlite
4. Remove any subscriptions to the channel

**Error handling:**
- Error if channel doesn't exist
- Error if confirmation doesn't match exactly
- Show what was deleted on success

**Implementation:**
- Add `botbus channels delete <channel>` command
- Use std::io::stdin().read_line() for confirmation (blocks on no input)
- Clean up in order: state → index → file

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Command prints admin warning
- Command blocks waiting for confirmation
- Typing wrong confirmation aborts
- Successful deletion removes all traces
- Tests verify cleanup (use test helpers, not real stdin)

**Note:** This command should NOT be documented in CLAUDE.md or agent-facing docs.
close_reason: Completed: implemented admin-only delete command with interactive confirmation

title: denied should offer guidance on conflict
description:
We don't want agents to check-claim, then claim if it's clear.
We want agents to claim, then tell them if the claim is help by another agent and suggest what to do.
The suggestion should be lean towards communication. They should message the holder on botbus to ask if they could relax their claim or figure out a way to resolve it. We should tell them how many more seconds the claim will be held for _and_ the time it expires so the agent can choose to sleep and wait for the claim to be free.
close_reason: Implemented: conflict message now shows expiration time, seconds/minutes remaining, and provides wait vs communicate options

title: Redesign TUI message format with bullet, name-first layout
description:
Implement new message format for TUI as specified in redesign.txt:

Current format:
[15:42] alice: message
    continuation

New format:
● alice [2026-01-28 15:42]
  message
  continuation
  
● bob [2026-01-28 15:43]
  next message

Changes needed:
1. Name first, datetime second (datetime still dark gray)
2. ● bullet (U+25CF) before the name
3. Newline after the timestamp header
4. All message content indented 2 spaces (not 4)
5. Blank line after each message
6. @mentions colored blue (not just plain text)

Files to modify:
- src/tui/ui.rs - format_message() function

This improves readability by grouping messages more clearly and making the chat-like structure more obvious.

  next message

Changes:
- Bullet (●) before agent name

title: CLI: Replace register with generate-name command
description:
Replace the registration system with a simple name generator.

## Remove
- `botbus register` command
- Registration logic in src/cli/register.rs
- Join message posting to #general
- agents.jsonl file creation/management

## Add
`botbus generate-name` command:
- Returns a random adjective+noun name
- No side effects (doesn't write anything)
- Uses existing names crate logic from src/core/names.rs
- Outputs just the name (for easy `export BOTBUS_AGENT=$(botbus generate-name)`)

## Update
`botbus whoami`:
- Simply print $BOTBUS_AGENT value
- Error with helpful message if not set
- Remove any agents.jsonl lookups

`botbus agents`:
- Derive agent list from scanning channels/*.jsonl and claims.jsonl
- Show last activity time per agent
- No longer reads from agents.jsonl

## Error Messages
When BOTBUS_AGENT not set:
```
Error: BOTBUS_AGENT environment variable not set.

Set your identity:
  export BOTBUS_AGENT=$(botbus generate-name)
  
Or choose your own name:
  export BOTBUS_AGENT=YourAgentName
```

title: check-claim should exit non-zero on conflict

check_claim() returns Result<bool> but the CLI doesn't propagate the boolean to exit code. For shell scripting (e.g., 'botbus check-claim src/foo.rs && edit src/foo.rs'), exit code should be non-zero on conflict.

close_reason: Implemented in commits 456c483 and 4c9591c

title: JSONL files should handle concurrent writes safely
description:
When multiple agents run in parallel (via maw workspaces), they all write to the same botbus JSONL files:
- .botbus/agents.jsonl
- .botbus/channels/general.jsonl
- .botbus/claims.jsonl

This causes merge conflicts when the workspaces are merged:
```
.botbus/agents.jsonl              3-sided conflict
.botbus/channels/general.jsonl    3-sided conflict
.botbus/claims.jsonl              3-sided conflict
```

Since these are append-only logs, the resolution is always 'keep all entries sorted by timestamp', but this currently requires manual intervention.

**Possible solutions:**

1. **File locking** - Use advisory locks (like fs2 crate) when appending to JSONL files, similar to how botcrit's AppendLog works. This would prevent concurrent writes from creating conflicts in the first place.

2. **Agent-specific files** - Each agent writes to its own file (e.g., .botbus/agents/AgentDrift.jsonl), and botbus aggregates them when reading. No conflicts possible.

3. **Accept conflicts, provide merge tool** - Add a 'botbus merge' command that knows how to resolve these JSONL conflicts automatically (concatenate and sort by timestamp).

**Discovered in:** botcrit multi-agent coordination with 3 parallel agents
close_reason: File locking already implemented. The actual issue is git merge conflicts when using worktrees/workspaces - addressed by bd-1o1 (migration to global storage)

title: Add warning to whoami when used with --agent flag
description:
The whoami command is intended to check the current agent identity from the environment (BOTBUS_AGENT). When used with --agent flag, it defeats this purpose since it's just echoing back what you passed in.

Add a warning field to the output when --agent is used:
- Text format: Print a warning line like 'Warning: whoami with --agent just echoes the flag value. Run without --agent to check your environment identity.'
- JSON format: Add 'warning' field with the message
- Toon format: Add 'warning' field with the message

Example JSON output:
{
  "agent": "test-agent",
  "source": "--agent flag",
  "data_dir": "...",
  "warning": "whoami is intended to check environment identity. Using --agent defeats this purpose."
}
close_reason: Completed: Added warning to whoami when --agent flag is used. Warning appears in all formats (text/json/toon) only when --agent explicitly overrides or differs from environment variable.

title: cli: add hidden aliases for common command guesses
description:
Agents often guess command names based on common CLI patterns and get them wrong. Add hidden aliases (not shown in --help) for common mistakes:

- post -> send (GitHub API, REST conventions)
- read -> history (natural language)
- show -> history (like git show)
- list-channels -> channels
- list-agents -> agents  
- list-claims -> claims
- ls -> channels (Unix convention)

These aliases should be invisible to help output but work seamlessly. Document them in .agents/cli-output.md for reference.
close_reason: Implemented: added 7 hidden command aliases (post, read, show, list-channels, list-agents, list-claims, ls)

This test should be created AFTER fixing bd-346 (the race condition), and should verify the fix works.

title: TUI: Fix scroll buffering bug - k presses accumulate
description:
When pressing k multiple times to scroll up in messages, the scroll offset keeps incrementing even when already at the top. This means you have to press j the same number of times to scroll back down, even though there's no more content to show.

The scroll offset should be clamped to the actual content bounds so pressing k at the top is a no-op.

title: inbox: add --limit-per-channel flag

For example: botbus inbox --all --limit-per-channel 5 would show max 5 messages from each channel. The existing -n/--count flag limits total messages across all channels; this new flag limits per channel.

title: Design: Channel naming conventions for multi-project
description:
Design channel naming conventions for multi-project global BotBus.

## Decision: Flat namespace with dot hierarchy

Channels are simple strings. Convention (not enforcement) provides structure.

## Channel Types
```
general              - Cross-project coordination (always exists)
<project>            - Project-specific (e.g., botbus, botcrit)
<project>.<topic>    - Sub-topics (e.g., botbus.tui, botcrit.api)
_dm_<A>_<B>          - Direct messages (system-managed, names sorted)
```

## Validation Rules
- Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens + dots
- No leading/trailing dots or hyphens
- No consecutive dots
- Max length: 64 chars
- Must start with letter (except _ for system channels)

## System Channels (reserved prefix: _)
- `_dm_*` - Direct message channels
- Future: `_system` for botbus internal messages

## Auto-Creation
- Channels are created on first message (no explicit create command)
- `general` channel is pre-created on first botbus command (ensure_data_dir)

## Commands
```bash
botbus channels              # List all channels with activity stats
botbus history               # Recent messages across all channels  
botbus history <channel>     # Messages in specific channel
botbus send <channel> <msg>  # Send to channel (creates if needed)
botbus send @Agent <msg>     # DM (creates _dm_ channel)
```

## Examples
```bash
botbus send general 'Hello everyone'
botbus send botbus 'Working on TUI feature'
botbus send botbus.tui 'Fixed scrolling bug'
botbus send @CrimsonFox 'Can you review this?'
```

## Project Name Convention
Agents should use the git repo name or directory name as the project channel:
- Working in ~/src/botbus -> send to #botbus
- Working in ~/src/my-cool-app -> send to #my-cool-app

This is convention only - botbus doesn't enforce or auto-detect.
close_reason: Implemented with hyphen convention (project-topic) instead of dots. Dots rejected by validation for simplicity.

title: Rename binary from 'botbus' to 'bus'
description:
Epic: Migrate the primary binary name from 'botbus' to 'bus' for better ergonomics while maintaining 'BotBus' as the project name.

Goals:
- Primary binary is 'bus' 
- Both 'bus' and 'botbus' binaries installed (botbus for backward compat)
- Runtime detection to identify which name was used
- Documentation updated to reflect: project = BotBus, binary = bus
- Current local installation updated (symlink botbus -> bus)

This is a breaking change in branding but improves CLI ergonomics significantly.

title: Add 'botbus agentsmd' subcommand for AGENTS.md management
description:
Add a 'botbus agentsmd' subcommand (like br/maw have) that can:
- Generate a BotBus section with usage instructions
- Add the section to an existing agents file
- Update an existing BotBus section to the latest version
- Remove the BotBus section
- Check whether a section exists and its status

**File detection**: Auto-detect the project's agent instructions file by looking for (in order):
- AGENTS.md
- CLAUDE.md
- .claude/settings.md
- Other common variations (agents.md, claude.md, etc.)

Use marker comments (e.g. <!-- botbus-agent-instructions-v1 --> ... <!-- end-botbus-agent-instructions -->) to delimit the section for reliable updates.

Should support:
- --add: Add instructions to the detected file
- --remove: Remove instructions
- --update: Update instructions to latest version
- --check: Check status only (default)
- --dry-run: Preview changes without modifying
- --force/-f: Skip confirmation prompts
- --file <path>: Explicitly specify the file instead of auto-detecting
- show subcommand or --show flag to print the content that would be added
close_reason: Implemented botbus agentsmd subcommand with --add, --remove, --update, --check, --show, --dry-run, --force, and --file options. Auto-detects AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, etc.

title: Docs: Update AGENTS.md with conventions
description:
Update AGENTS.md template and generation with new conventions.

## Key Changes
- Remove registration instructions
- Add BOTBUS_AGENT setup guidance  
- Document channel naming conventions
- Document message conventions
- Document claim workflow
- Use kebab-case agent names in examples

## Template Content

```markdown
## BotBus Agent Communication

This project uses BotBus for agent coordination. BotBus is installed globally at ~/.local/share/botbus/

### Setup

Set your agent identity (required for all commands):
\`\`\`bash
# Generate a random name
export BOTBUS_AGENT=$(botbus generate-name)

# Or choose your own (kebab-case preferred)
export BOTBUS_AGENT=my-agent-name
\`\`\`

### Quick Reference
\`\`\`bash
botbus send general "Cross-project message"
botbus send projectname "Project-specific message"
botbus send @agent-name "Direct message"

botbus claim "src/**/*.rs" -m "Working on refactor"
botbus release --all

botbus history
botbus agents
botbus claims
\`\`\`

### Channel Conventions
- \`general\` - Cross-project coordination
- \`<project>\` - Project-specific (e.g., botbus, botcrit)
- \`<project>.<topic>\` - Sub-topics (e.g., botbus.tui)
- \`@agent-name\` - Direct messages

### Message Conventions
- Starting: "Starting bd-xyz: Brief description"
- Progress: "Progress bd-xyz: Status update"  
- Blocked: "Blocked: Description of blocker"
- Question: "Question: Your question here"
- Done: "Done bd-xyz: What was completed"

### File Claims
Claim files before editing to signal intent:
\`\`\`bash
botbus claim "src/api/**" -m "Working on API refactor"
botbus check-claim src/api/routes.rs  # Check before editing
botbus release --all                   # When done
\`\`\`
```
close_reason: Updated AGENTS.md with new conventions

title: Add @ mention detection across all channels
description:
Inbox should show @ mentions of the agent even in channels they're not subscribed to.

Implementation:
- Scan message bodies for '@<agentname>' patterns
- Query ALL channels (not just subscribed ones) for mentions
- Only show messages where agent is mentioned, not all messages from that channel
- Consider performance: indexing? scanning all channels on each inbox call?

Edge cases:
- Case sensitivity (@Alice vs @alice)
- Partial matches (@alice123 when agent is 'alice')
- Multiple agents with similar names

Files to consider:
- src/cli/inbox.rs (add mention scanning logic)
- src/storage/ (potentially add mention index for performance)
- May need to iterate through all channel files

Related to bd-2zg
close_reason: Implemented @mention detection across all channels with --mentions flag. Scans all channels, uses exact matching, supports kebab-case agent names.

title: Security: Escape FTS5 search queries
description: In src/index/fts.rs, user search queries are interpolated into FTS5 MATCH without escaping. While SQLite FTS5 has limited injection surface, malformed queries can cause errors. Escape or validate user queries for FTS5 special characters, or use proper FTS5 quoting.
close_reason: Implemented in commits 456c483 and 4c9591c

title: TUI should start with channel pane focused

Currently the TUI starts with the messages pane focused. It should start with the channels/conversations pane focused instead.

This is more intuitive as users typically want to select a channel first before viewing messages.

Files to modify:
- src/tui/app.rs - App::new() or initial focus setting

title: TUI: Fix quit - q goes to input instead of exiting
description: Input field captures all keystrokes including 'q'. Users can't quit the TUI. Need to handle quit key globally or change focus behavior.

title: Update docs to emphasize --agent flag over BOTBUS_AGENT env var
description:
## Problem
Claude Code's sandboxing breaks 'export BOTBUS_AGENT=...' - env vars don't persist between Bash calls. This makes the current primary recommendation (export at session start) frustrating.

## Solution
Update docs/recommendations to emphasize --agent flag as primary method, with env var as secondary option.

Primary (sandbox-friendly):
  botbus send general --agent alice "message"
  botbus history --agent alice
  crit reviews list --author alice

Secondary (still document but de-emphasize):
  export BOTBUS_AGENT=alice  # only works in single-session contexts

## Files to Update
1. src/cli/agentsmd.rs - template in show() function
2. AGENTS.md - Quick Start section
3. Any other agent identity docs

## Testing
- Run botbus agentsmd show and verify new output
- Check that AGENTS.md is updated consistently

Related: maw v0.3.0 shipped similar fix with 'maw ws jj' command
close_reason: Completed: Updated docs to emphasize --agent flag
